_meta.author: Claude (national autopilot, batch 2026-06-05 0900)
_meta.note: CA-48 (Issa open seat) cleanup post 2026-06-02 top-two primary. Five 2026 D/NPP filers: von Wilpert advanced (vs Desmond R) — evidence-finalized; Bucy, Davis (slug nicholas-davis-ca-48), Till, Shanahan all withdrew or did not qualify for the ballot — set status=lost / candidacy_status=not_running so they leave the active pool. Sources: CA SoS official primary results, FEC filings, KPBS, East County Magazine, Times of San Diego, Brady PAC, Equality CA, LGBTQ Victory Fund, EMILYs List, Ballotpedia.

set.office: U.S. Representative CA-48 (2026 D general candidate, vs Desmond R · SD City Councilmember District 5)

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of personal faith identification; co-sponsored a 2026 state ask for $40M security funding for 'religious institutions and nonprofits' (religion-neutral)
profile.background: San Diego City Councilmember District 5 since 2020-12-10 (re-elected unopposed 2024); chairs SD Council Public Safety Committee. Prior: SD Deputy City Attorney 2018-2020; NLRB attorney; House Education & Labor staffer for Rep. Bobby Scott; Economic Policy Institute; Peace Corps Botswana (HIV/AIDS). Born 1983 San Diego (Scripps Ranch). Openly LGBTQ+; engaged.
profile.education: Scripps Ranch HS (2001); UC Berkeley BA Peace & Conflict Studies; Fordham Law (JD)

marni-von-wilpert.notes_set: CA-48 (Issa open seat) 2026 D general candidate; advanced from 2026-06-02 top-two primary (D 19.5% / R Desmond 41.4%) to face Jim Desmond (R) on Nov 3 2026. Sitting SD City Councilmember District 5 since Dec 2020. [2026-06-05 evidence-finalize, federal tier] Conventional CA progressive Dem: explicit pro-Roe (federal Roe codification quote), authored 2022 SD Council 'Safe City for Reproductive Freedom' resolution, EMILYs List endorsed → sanctity_of_life FALSE; openly LGBTQ+ (engaged to girlfriend), Victory Fund + Equality CA endorsed, pushes Equality Act → biblical_marriage FALSE; co-authored 2026 SD Council anti-ICE resolution + supports pathways for Afghans/Dreamers → border_immigration FALSE; authored SD 'ENUF Ordinance' ghost-gun ban + Ira Sharp Firearm Dealer Accountability Act, Brady/Giffords/Moms Demand endorsed → self_defense FALSE. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION (the high-value catch): explicit Article I war-powers stance on Iran ('opposing unilateral military action without congressional approval') + campaign-site bullet 'Oppose Trump's forever wars' → foreign_policy_restraint 0/1 TRUE. Counterweight: DMFI PAC endorsed + AIPAC Tracker logs 'unlimited weapons for Israel' stance + StandWithUs speaker → foreign_policy_restraint 3 FALSE (so the 'restraint' is partisan-selective, not principled isolationism). All other federal cells null pending direct evidence.

0.note: Stated goal: 'restore the rights of Roe v. Wade across this country and make it a national federal law.' Does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Authored the 2022 SD City Council 'Safe City for Reproductive Freedom' resolution: 'the City of San Diego will not stand for an assault on women's rights.' Active pro-abortion-access posture, not abortion abolition.

4.note: EMILYs List endorsement (abortion-industry money trigger per rubric). Also self-stated: 'make sure that Planned Parenthoods are funded' (East County Mag).

0.note: Engaged to her girlfriend, wedding planned 2027: 'I just got engaged to my girlfriend. We are planning to get married next year.' Does not affirm 1-man-1-woman marriage.

1.note: LGBTQ+ Victory Fund + Equality California endorsements (Victory Fund endorses only out LGBTQ+ candidates committed to LGBTQ-rights advancement); engaged to same-sex partner. Hard evidence of same-sex marriage support.

4.note: Self-stated: 'get the Equality Act in Congress so that people who face discrimination, who face homophobia, they need to have the same rights as everyone else.' Active advocate for LGBTQ-rights legislation.

0.note: Frames the border as 'strong border that stops bad actors and drugs and guns, but promotes lawful immigration and has a pathway for citizenship' — does not endorse completed wall or military border presence.

1.note: Supports legalization pathways for Afghan refugees and Dreamers; authored 'Resolution in Support of the Afghan Adjustment Act.' Opposes mandatory deportation.

2.note: Co-sponsored and passed 2026-02-02 SD Council resolution opposing ICE enforcement tactics and authorizing the City Attorney to join lawsuits against Trump-admin ICE enforcement. Quote: 'ICE is not law enforcement, it's chaos.' Sanctuary-aligned posture.

0.note: Authored San Diego 'ENUF Ordinance' — California's first municipal ban on untraceable ghost guns. Directly opposed to permitless/constitutional carry frame.

1.note: 'I am a strong proponent of gun violence prevention.' Authored Ira Sharp Firearm Dealer Accountability Act.

4.note: Endorsed by Brady PAC, Giffords PAC, and Moms Demand Action — full gun-control trifecta per rubric (HARD evidence trigger).

0.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: explicit Article I stance on Iran — 'opposing unilateral military action without congressional approval'; called Trump's Iran approach 'a complete quagmire.'

1.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION (partial / Trump-framed): campaign-site bullet 'Oppose Trump's forever wars.' Counterweight in cell 3 — full pro-Israel-arms posture — so the restraint is partisan-selective, not principled isolationism.

3.note: Endorsed by DMFI PAC (Democratic Majority for Israel) and on the StandWithUs speaker list; AIPAC Tracker (@TrackAIPAC) records 'supports unlimited weapons for Israel.' Stated: 'Israel's right to exist as a secure, Jewish democratic state and defend itself from the real threats it faces.' Foreign-government-aligned-PAC support per rubric trigger.

set.office: U.S. Representative CA-48 (2026 NPP filer · did NOT qualify for ballot · FEC filing was CA-51)

profile.background: FEC Statement of Candidacy filed 2025-08-14 (committee 'Bucy for Congress 2026,' FEC ID H6CA51116, party listed Independent American Party). Filing was for CA-51, not CA-48; Ballotpedia also lists a declaration in CA-48. Residence per FEC: El Cajon, CA. Did NOT appear on the certified 2026-06-02 California primary ballot in either CA-48 or CA-51.

mike-bucy.notes_set: CA-48 2026 NPP filer record. [2026-06-05 evidence-finalize, federal tier] Did NOT qualify for the 2026-06-02 California primary ballot in either CA-48 or CA-51 per CA SoS official results. FEC SoC was for CA-51 (H6CA51116, 2025-08-14, committee 'Bucy for Congress 2026'); Ballotpedia separately lists a declaration for CA-48. No campaign website, no discoverable socials specific to this candidate (the @BucyForTexas handles belong to John Bucy III, unrelated), no Ballotpedia Candidate Connection response, no policy positions in public record. Status set to lost so the record exits the active pool; all 50 federal cells null (no evidence to score in any direction). CA-48 general advancers: Desmond (R) and von Wilpert (D).

set.office: U.S. Representative CA-48 (2026 D filer · did NOT qualify for ballot)

profile.background: FEC Statement of Candidacy filed 2025-07-03 (committee 'Friends of Nick,' FEC ID H6CA48294, C00910216). Did NOT appear on the certified 2026-06-02 California primary ballot for CA-48; absent from CA SoS results, SD County Democratic Party 2026 candidate list, and major press roundups (KPBS, CBS8, CalMatters, Times of San Diego). No campaign website, no discoverable socials, no Ballotpedia Candidate Connection survey response. Do not conflate with Pastor Nicholas Martin Davis (Redemption PCA San Marcos) — different person.

nicholas-davis-ca-48.notes_set: CA-48 2026 D filer record. [2026-06-05 evidence-finalize, federal tier] Did NOT qualify for the 2026-06-02 California primary ballot for CA-48 per CA SoS official results. FEC SoC filed 2025-07-03 (H6CA48294, committee 'Friends of Nick'); FEC financial summary 'still being processed.' Absent from SD County Democratic Party candidate list and all major press coverage. No public policy positions, no Issues page, no questionnaire response on record. Status set to lost so the record exits the active pool; all 50 federal cells null (no evidence). Slug suffix preserved to prevent collision with other Nicholas Davis records nationally. CA-48 general advancers: Desmond (R) and von Wilpert (D).

set.office: U.S. Representative CA-48 (2026 D filer · WITHDREW pre-primary · endorsed von Wilpert)

profile.religion: Catholic — parishioner at Our Lady of Perpetual Help; teaches at Mater Dei Catholic High School (Chula Vista)
profile.background: Retired USMC Captain (commissioned 1984, Operation Desert Storm, Camp Pendleton adjutant); geography teacher at Mater Dei Catholic HS; former watershed coordinator, Southern California Wetlands Recovery Project; Director, Padre Dam Municipal Water District Division 2 (elected 2020, re-elected 2024); board Vice President. Born College Park Hospital (East SD), raised Lakeside CA. Lung cancer 2022, in remission.
profile.education: El Capitan HS (Lakeside); UCLA BA; SDSU MA (geography/urban planning); University of Colorado Boulder PhD (water resources geography)

suzanne-till.notes_set: CA-48 2026 D filer record. [2026-06-05 evidence-finalize, federal tier] WITHDREW from CA-48 pre-2026-06-02 primary (per Ballotpedia and her own pre-pledged 'no CA Dem Party endorsement = drop out' condition) and endorsed Marni von Wilpert (D). Now markets a candidacy for CA State Senate District 40 on her campaign site, but does NOT appear on 2026-06-02 SS-40 primary results (Elliott D / Bruce-Lane R / Musgrove R advanced) — current candidacy in any race is ambiguous. Sitting Padre Dam Municipal Water District director (Division 2). Catholic (Our Lady of Perpetual Help; teacher at Mater Dei Catholic HS); retired USMC Captain. Thin federal-tier policy record on her CA-48 campaign: documented anti-ICE-in-schools stance (anti-sanctuary-policy cell → FALSE) and 'immigrants must not be treated as criminals' framing → border_immigration 1/2 FALSE; pro-ACA/Medicaid/Medicare posture; otherwise no public position on the conservative rubric's cells (life, marriage, guns, election integrity, foreign policy, pharma) — cells null. Status set to lost so the record exits the active CA-48 pool. CA-48 general advancers: Desmond (R) and von Wilpert (D).

1.note: Self-stated: 'Immigrants and immigrant families must not be portrayed as or treated as criminals.' Pro-pathway framing; opposes mandatory deportation.

2.note: Priorities page commits to ensuring 'ICE and DHS cannot be on or near our schools' and training educators 'to protect our students from ICE raids' — sanctuary-aligned local policy.

set.office: U.S. Representative CA-48 (2026 D filer · did NOT qualify for ballot)

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of personal faith identification
profile.background: National abortion-rights advocate; founder of 'Pro Choice with Heart' (TikTok @whitneywithheart ~500K followers); consultant/content creator. Mother of one young son; lives in Fallbrook, CA. Also ran 2024 CA-48 D primary — did not advance. Distinct from Nicole Shanahan (RFK Jr. running mate).
profile.education: Bachelor's degree, Bowling Green State University (2009)

whitney-shanahan.notes_set: CA-48 2026 D filer record. [2026-06-05 evidence-finalize, federal tier] Did NOT qualify for the 2026-06-02 California primary ballot for CA-48 per CA SoS official results; Vote-USA lists her as 'Previous Candidate'; FEC raised only ~$4,174 / spent ~$3,033 through Mar 2026 (FEC H4CA48075). Also did not advance from 2024 CA-48 D primary. Single-issue brand: national abortion-rights advocate, founder of #ProChoiceWithHeart, 270K+ signature petition to defend Roe, hundreds of pro-choice rallies → sanctity_of_life 0/1 FALSE; 'common-sense gun prevention' framing rooted in losing her brother to gun violence → self_defense 1 FALSE; 'simplifying pathways to citizenship' / 'family unity' → border_immigration 1 FALSE; 'backs Ukraine with proportional response' → foreign_policy_restraint 1 FALSE. Other federal cells null pending direct evidence. Status set to lost so the record exits the active CA-48 pool. CA-48 general advancers: Desmond (R) and von Wilpert (D).

0.note: Self-described 'national abortion rights advocate'; founder of #ProChoiceWithHeart; stated goal to 'reinstate the protections of Roe v Wade to ensure nationwide protection to reproductive rights in all 50 states.' Does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Led 'hundreds of Pro-Choice rallies … to DEFEND ROE'; 270,000+ signature petition to defend abortion rights. Active pro-abortion-access advocate, not pro-abolition.

Top-priority campaign issue 'ending gun violence'; bio frames it as 'lost her brother to gun violence as a child and now that she is a mother will work to keep our families and kids safe in congress with common sense prevention.' Standard gun-control framing.
'Supports simplifying pathways to citizenship'; advocates 'family unity and safety for women/children.' Opposes mandatory deportation.
'Backs Ukraine with proportional response' — supports continued U.S. involvement, not withdrawal from forever wars / AUMF repeal.
